title: Lead Targeting Fundamentals
summary: Calculate aim point ahead of moving targets based on projectile travel time.
details: Projectiles take time to reach the target. Aim at where the target will be when the projectile arrives, not where it is now. Lead distance = target_velocity * (distance / projectile_velocity). Account for both target and own ship movement. Faster targets require more lead. Closer targets require less lead. Update lead calculation continuously as engagement parameters change.
annotations: Fundamental gunnery skill - practice until intuitive.
Most targeting computers handle this automatically but understanding helps.
